title: Dress codes decoded: invisible is the goal
microFact: The dress-code winner isn't the flashiest outfit — it's the one nobody remembers. Collared, tucked, tailored.
lesson:
Indian club dress codes are conservative and enforced. The safe uniform, valid from Clover Greens to KGA to any club in the country:

• COLLARED polo (or mock-neck golf shirt), TUCKED IN — always
• Tailored shorts (near the knee) or chinos/golf trousers — never denim, cargo shorts, gym shorts, or track pants
• Golf shoes, spikeless fine, CLEAN — no runners, no sandals
• Cap or visor, brim FORWARD; remove it indoors and for the 18th-green handshake
• Socks with shorts (short crew is current; the old rule at stuffier clubs was long whites — when in doubt, just wear proper socks)

CLUBHOUSE rules are separate and stricter: many Indian clubs bar caps, and sometimes shorts, in certain dining rooms. Glance at what members do, or simply ask — asking the dress code when booking is itself a fluent move, not an embarrassing one.

Spending guide: two or three quality polos that FIT beat seven loud ones. Loud trousers, tour-pro logos head-to-toe, and rangefinder-on-the-belt-at-dinner all read as costume. Understatement reads as membership.

quiz.question: Which item is a near-universal NO at Indian golf clubs?
options: A forward-facing cap on the course
quiz.explanation: Denim is the classic dress-code violation, on course and often in the clubhouse. Collared, tucked, tailored, clean shoes — invisible.
